title: dodonaea humilis
intro: Dodonaea humilis, also known as the small-leaved hop bush, is a small tree or shrub native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its small leaves and clusters of small flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dodonaea humilis include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting immune function, as well as providing protection against infections and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Dodonaea humilis can be made by infusing the leaves and branches in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's extracts in capsule or tincture form.
